title: Spec review session: commit and push completed changes
Wire up specify create command + narrative format
description: COMPLETED. All 7 items done: routing, manifest, narrative_path type+schema, self-spec tests, help text, e2e flow.

title: Product identity: deterministic CLI vs LLM-native hybrid
description: DECIDED: Agent-first. Specify stays fully deterministic. LLMs interact via MCP server or CLI. No API keys required. Designed for symbiosis with existing agents.

title: Narrative ↔ spec sync: keep human and computable specs aligned
description:
COMPLETED. Implemented:
- spec lint: narrative-ref-invalid, narrative-ref-missing, narrative-missing rules (triggered when narrative_path is set)
- spec evolve: analyzeNarrative() produces suggestions for stale refs, missing coverage, and missing narrative_path (in both interactive and PR modes)
- specify review: stale ref highlighting in browser (orange dot in TOC, strikethrough on ref tags, warning indicator)
- loadNarrativeForSpec() auto-discovers narrative files

title: Spec browser: specify review --spec <path>
description: COMPLETED. Self-contained HTML spec browser with narrative view, spec toggle, validation overlay, stale section highlighting, auto-open browser, spec auto-discovery.

title: Capture auto-generates spec
description: COMPLETED. specify capture --url <url> --output <dir> now auto-generates a spec from captured data after capture completes. Use --no-generate to skip. Additional flags: --spec-output, --spec-name.

title: Fork worker pool for parallel verify concurrency
description: Replace single-worker serialization in src/daemon/inbox.ts with a child_process.fork-based worker pool. Parent keeps inbox queue; N worker processes each own their Playwright+Chromium and runSpecifyAgent invocation. Route events back over IPC tagged with msgId. Attach-mode sessions claim a worker for their lifetime. --max-workers flag on `specify daemon`. Preserve SSE stream contract (sessionId-tagged events).

title: Learned-memory system for Specify verify agent
description: Four-tier memory inspired by Learning runtime: session store (raw traces), skills/playbooks (how-to procedures), entity model (per-target quirks), nudged persistence (post-run reflection). SQLite at .specify/memory.db. Rows keyed by (spec_id, target_env, behavior_id?). Read-path injects matching rows in verify prompts capped at ~2KB. Write-path: post-run agent extracts durable lessons. Staleness via last_confirmed_run_id + contradicted_count. JSON export for git-diff. Scope rules: (spec,target) primary; optional secondary (spec,*) or (*,origin); never both wildcards.

title: Quirk triage: file-and-continue pattern
description: Agent encounters unexpected behavior during verify, writes a quirk row with suggested_fix + severity, continues rather than blocking. End-of-run report surfaces all open quirks for the (spec, target) pair. Depends on SP-d02 learned-memory system.

title: UI: agent introspection panel
description: Review webapp currently shows specs + verify results. Add live agent introspection: current message, tool calls, thought/text stream, cost, session state. Use /events/stream SSE from daemon. Panel should work for both foreground `specify verify` runs and daemon-dispatched jobs.
